text:
1. It has to actually work; it has to succeed in all of the tests.
2. 50% of it has to go to charity.
3. No government involvement at all and this is how we will know that we are actually a private company and not a public one.
4. Actually cancel step number four; that's fine.
5. We have to build logical graphs around it.

1. Prepare the release branch.
2. Validate the payment flow
   across sandbox and production paths.
3. Publish the changelog.

• Fix the crash on startup.
• Recheck the permission copy.
• Confirm analytics are still firing.

- Audit the text fields.
- Verify the keyboard survives app switching.
- Measure latency after warmup.

1) Define the scope.
2) Freeze the benchmark.
3) Iterate until the metrics stop moving.
2026.03.11 was the date on the invoice, not a numbered step list.
Version 1.2 shipped before 2.0, and both releases were messy.
1.5 hours later we finally got an answer.

The checklist was delayed.
Someone wrote 1. wrong room on the whiteboard, which confused everyone.
The shortlist now includes pricing fixes, onboarding polish, support macros, and refund tooling.
She looked at the chart, the room went quiet, and the meeting ended.
